rationale: Design-fidelity lens: the adapter design is faithfully and completely captured. Every agreed point is present and internally consistent — workflow as the first runner adapter behind 110's contracts (R1-R5), decision authority unchanged (R5 + ADR-0037 touchpoint: never runs `mindspec complete`, never consolidates, never writes consolidated-round-N.md), codex-as-wrapper-agents grounded in the documented 'no shell/fs access from the script' limit (R3), config-driven substitution via `panel.substitution.claude_sub_on_quota` keeping slot id + `claude-sub` reviewer_id (R4), artifact-based re-rounds via `panel create --round N+1` not workflow resume (R2/OpenQ), skills-path default gated-not-deleted (R6/R7), capability tiers honored (R8, Claude target only). I verified against the repo: `workflow` OWNERSHIP claims `.claude/skills/**` but not `.claude/workflows/**` (R9 premise correct), the spec-108 `TestWorkflowOwnsTraceAndGolangci` precedent exists, and every impacted path resolves to a `workflow` glob (sole-domain claim holds). Nothing dropped. One 'nothing-invented' hit: the Background cites `project-docs/research/loop-engineering-adaptation.md` §3.2/§3.3 with verbatim quotes, but that file is absent from the worktree, main, all branches, and history (only `scaling-agent-systems.md` exists). Unverifiable citation in a spec whose thesis is 'integrate at verifiable contracts' — reconcile, but it gates no requirement or acceptance criterion, so non-blocking for approval.
concrete_changes_required: Reconcile the Background research citation (spec.md line 22): either commit `project-docs/research/loop-engineering-adaptation.md` so the cited §3.2/§3.3 quotes resolve, or soften the reference (drop the verbatim quotes + section numbers, or point to the actually-tracked research artifact) so the spec does not cite a file that is absent from the repo and all of git history. Design-only; does not touch any Requirement, Scope boundary, or Acceptance Criterion.
findings: FIDELITY OK: R5 + ADR-0037/Non-Goals/Open-Questions consistently hold the 'adapter, not second decision authority' invariant — workflow returns `panel verify`/`panel tally` output as its result but never runs `mindspec complete`, never does semantic consolidation, never authors consolidated-round-N.md. Matches the agreed decision-authority-unchanged design.

FIDELITY OK: R3 codex-wrapper-agent design (codex never writes; the agent execs `codex exec`, parses stdout, writes the verdict itself) correctly derives from the documented workflow limit and eliminates the sandbox-file-write + verdict-parse failure classes by construction rather than assuming a platform output-schema guarantee (Background/Non-Goals are explicit about not assuming one).

FIDELITY OK: R4 substitution is a config-driven branch (`claude_sub_on_quota` true→sub keeping slot id + `<slot> claude-sub`; false→leave missing so the gate Blocks), i.e. policy-not-judgment on the workflow path — faithful to the portability/loop-engineering framing regardless of the citation provenance.

FIDELITY OK: R6/R7 retain the skills-path default (gated behind the runner branch, not deleted), keep the Slot lens defaults judgment section, and keep all named ms-panel-tally judgment sections — nothing over-slimmed.

FIDELITY OK: R8/R9 verified against repo — capability-tiered Claude-only distribution mirrors the SkillFiles()/installSkills pattern; `.claude/workflows/**` is genuinely unclaimed today (confirmed in workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml), so the same-diff-or-earlier claim (spec-108 invariant) is real, not decorative.

MINOR (traceability, non-blocking): 109 and 110 are not on this worktree's base (they are declared hard prerequisites landing before plan-approve), so their `runner:` key / `panel` verbs / verdict schema could not be directly verified here — but this is disclosed openly and mirrors the spec-108→107 ordering pattern, so it is not an invention.

OBSERVED (not a fidelity defect): the only research file actually in project-docs/research/ is `scaling-agent-systems.md`, which is about bead decomposition (agent-count sweet spot, redundancy) — unrelated to the §3.2 'panel launch is interactive-agent-shaped' material the spec quotes. This is what makes the missing citation notable rather than a mere path typo.
